pattern: Counting pairs with a sum inside a range decomposes into two counts of pairs with a sum at most a bound, each solvable with a single two-pointer sweep over a sorted copy of the array.
hints: Sorting the array doesn't change how many pairs of VALUES satisfy the sum condition, since you're counting unordered pairs, not specific positions.

Write a helper that counts pairs whose sum is at most some bound using two pointers from both ends of the sorted array, moving the low pointer inward and adding the whole remaining window whenever a pair fits.
The count of pairs with sum in [lower, upper] is exactly (pairs with sum <= upper) minus (pairs with sum <= lower - 1).

edge_inputs.description: single element array can never form a pair
every value identical, many pairs share the same sum
lower and upper collapse to a single exact target sum
extreme negative values at the constraint minimum
range wide enough that every pair qualifies
mixed positive and negative values with a narrow window that excludes most pairs

stress.description: random values across the full range with a random qualifying window
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    a = rng.randint(-2000000000, 2000000000)
    b = rng.randint(-2000000000, 2000000000)
    lower, upper = (a, b) if a <= b else (b, a)
    return [nums, lower, upper]

oracle_python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def countFairPairs(self, nums, lower, upper):
        nums = sorted(nums)
        n = len(nums)
        total = 0
        for i in range(n):
            lo = bisect.bisect_left(nums, lower - nums[i], i + 1, n)
            hi = bisect.bisect_right(nums, upper - nums[i], i + 1, n)
            total += hi - lo
        return total
